code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test15-FEB-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, ketonuria, and glucosuria, with low urine specific gravity and low HbA1c. These findings suggest possible acute or chronic renal impairment and impaired glucose handling, but without evidence of infection. The brown, cloudy urine with ammoniacal odor may indicate underlying renal or hepatic dysfunction. Further clinical correlation and assessment of renal and hepatic function are recommended.
